question_text: A wooden block has a density of 0.6 g/cm³ and water has a density of 1.0 g/cm³. Explain why the wooden block will float in water.
correct_answer: The wooden block has a lower density than water, so it is less dense. Objects that are less dense than the liquid they are placed in will float because they are lighter for their size.
explanation: Objects float when their density is lower than the density of the liquid they are in. Since wood (0.6 g/cm³) is less dense than water (1.0 g/cm³), the wood floats to the surface.
